## UTC Conversion & Timeline

| # | Source | Local Time | Timezone | → UTC |
|---|--------|-----------|----------|-------|
| 1 | Auth server | 06:28 | UTC-8 (PST) | **14:28** |
| 2 | DNS server | 14:30 | UTC | **14:30** |
| 3 | Firewall | 14:32 | UTC | **14:32** |
| 4 | Web server | 09:35 | UTC-5 (EST) | **14:35** |
| 5 | Database | 09:38 | UTC-5 (EST) | **14:38** |
| 6 | Email server | 15:41 | UTC+1 (CET) | **14:41** |

**FIRST:** Auth server — failed login attempts at **14:28 UTC**
**LAST:** Email server — exfiltration email at **14:41 UTC**

The full attack played out in just **13 minutes** and follows a textbook kill chain: reconnaissance (DNS lookup) → brute-force auth → firewall breach → web exploit → database access → data exfiltration via email.
